name: Rolls-Royce Motor Cars
philosophy: An inspired interpretation of Rolls-Royce Motor Cars — the British ultra-luxury house at the apex of automotive prestige, where bespoke commissions average several million pounds and 'car' is the wrong word for the object on display. Canvas is bone-cream (#f5f1e9) or velvet-black (#0a0a0a) depending on whether the section showcases craft (cream) or product (black). Type runs a refined serif (Riviera Nights / custom Trajan-adjacent display serif) at editorial scale, paired with a precise grotesk body. The signature is the Spirit of Ecstasy hood ornament — the silver Eleanor Thornton sculpture rendered in delicate line art on every page. Photography is gallery-grade: hand-stitched leather close-ups, starlight-headliner-roof shots, marquetry inlay macro photography. The voice is 'effortless mastery' — never claims, never promotes, always implies.

design_language.motion_signature: Almost-zero motion. Hero photography cross-fades over 3 seconds — the slowest in the automotive category. The Spirit of Ecstasy ornament has a subtle hover-shimmer that takes 1.2s to complete — barely perceptible. The brand voice is 'time is the ultimate luxury' — motion would imply hurry.

trademark_signals: Bone-cream canvas (#f5f1e9) with serif typography at editorial scale — refusing the modernist-grotesk convention every other automotive brand has adopted
Spirit of Ecstasy ornament rendered in delicate silver line art — appears on every page as a quiet sub-mark, never large, never animated
Roman-inscriptional display serif (Riviera Nights / Trajan-adjacent) at 60–120px — the typeface IS the brand voice; sans-serif would read as Mercedes off-brand
Macro-photography of craft details — hand-stitched leather seams, marquetry inlay, starlight-headliner LEDs, monogrammed treadplates — bespoke craft IS the visual content
'Effortless' as a recurring word in the voice — paired with quiet observations about silence, weight, hand-finishing — never feature lists, never spec sheets
ai_slop_avoided: Don't substitute the display serif with a modern grotesk. The Roman-inscriptional serif IS the brand voice — sans reads as Mercedes/Porsche, off-brand for Rolls-Royce
Don't paint accent moments with saturated color. The palette is bone-cream + ink + restrained gold; saturated colors read as off-brand
Don't show interior shots populated by people. The brand photographs the empty cabin — the unoccupied luxury is the promise
Don't write feature-bullet marketing copy. Every product description is atmospheric prose about silence, weight, leather, marquetry — never spec sheets
Don't drop the Spirit of Ecstasy. Even in minimalist layouts, the silver line-art ornament appears somewhere — its absence reads as off-brand
